BusinessInstance.title: BusinessInstance
BusinessInstance.description: Stores core details for each business tenant, including their subscription status and settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BusinessInstance entity.

address.description: The physical address of the business.

subscriptionStatus.description: The current subscription status of the business.

settings.description: Settings object, holds configuration for the business instance.

slug.description: The URL-friendly slug for the public store.

industry.description: The industry the business operates in.

language.description: The default language for the business.

fiscalYearStart.description: The starting month of the fiscal year.

state.description: The state or province of the business.

User.title: User
User.description: Stores all user profiles. Each user has a businessId and a role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: The role of the user within the business.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of when the user was last active in the app.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: The main inventory collection, storing product details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit for the product, also used for barcode generation.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The selling price of the product.

costPrice.description: The cost of acquiring the product, used for profit calculation.

expiryDate.description: The expiration date of the product, for perishable goods.

Receipt.title: Receipt
Receipt.description: Stores records of all completed sales transactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Receipt entity.

date.description: The date and time when the sale was completed.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the sale.

paymentMethod.description: The method of payment used (e.g., Cash, Card).

taxRate.description: Tax rate applied to the sale.

lineItems.description: List of products bought in the sale.

totalCost.description: The total cost of goods sold for this transaction.

profit.description: The calculated profit for this transaction (Total - Total Cost).

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: The CRM database storing customer information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

Invitation.title: Invitation
Invitation.description: Stores pending invitations for users to join a business.

email.description: The email of the user being invited.

role.description: The role to be assigned to the user upon joining.

businessId.description: The ID of the business the user is invited to join.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was created.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents a successful subscription purchase transaction.

SubscriptionHistory.title: Subscription History
SubscriptionHistory.description: Logs an instance of a subscription payment for a business.

BlogPost.title: Blog Post
BlogPost.description: Represents a single article in the blog.

AdminNotification.title: Admin Notification
AdminNotification.description: A platform-wide notification sent to users.

SupportThread.title: Support Thread
SupportThread.description: Represents a single conversation thread between a user and admin support.

SupportMessage.title: Support Message
SupportMessage.description: A single message within a support thread.

AuditLog.title: Audit Log
AuditLog.description: An immutable log of important actions taken within a business instance.

definition.description: Stores core details for each business tenant, including their subscription status and settings.

params.name: businessId
params.description: Unique identifier for the BusinessInstance entity.

definition.description: Stores all user profiles. Requires the 'businessId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: productId

Stores a record of every successful subscription purchase for admin analytics.
A log of each subscription payment made for a business.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Zeneva is designed around multi-tenancy and the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. Each top-level collection (products, receipts, customers, and users) is scoped to a BusinessInstance to ensure data isolation and straightforward security rules. The design denormalizes the businessId into every business-specific document, thus ensuring authorization independence. The roles of users is stored directly in the `users` collection. Path-based ownership is applied to the `/users/{userId}` collection, simplifying security rules for user profiles.

The structure achieves Authorization Independence (Strategy A) by denormalizing the `businessId` into each document within the `products`, `receipts`, `customers` collections. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules, enabling atomic operations and improving performance. Every collection is segregated to guarantee a Homogeneous Security Posture (Strategy B).

A top-level `/purchases` collection is added for admins to easily query and analyze revenue data, while user-specific subscription details are nested under `/businessInstances/{businessId}/subscription_history` for privacy.

The referral system and its related notifications have been removed to simplify the application and resolve permission errors.

A new `auditLogs` subcollection is added under `businessInstances` to store immutable records of important actions, secured so that only admins of that business can read them and all authenticated users of that business can write to them.
